channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Senator Bong Go urges completion and full equipping of Super Health Centers amid reports of missing utilities
item.description: Senator Bong Go, vice chairman of the Senate committee on health, expressed serious concern over the incomplete status of several Super Health Centers across the country. During a recent committee hea...

item.title: GSM to Build Modern 20,000-Seat Stadium for Yanga
item.description: The CEO of GSM Group, Aisha Mohamed, has announced plans for a new 20,000-seater stadium for Young Africans (Yanga) football club in Dar es Salaam. She described the project as more than just a footba...

item.title: Senate Debates PTI Founder Imran Khan’s Health and Political Prisoner Access
item.description: On Friday, the Senate saw tense discussions concerning the health of PTI founder Imran Khan and the conditions of political prisoners in Pakistan. Opposition senators, including Azam Swati and Allama ...

item.title: Max Schödl: Austrian Painter Known for Detailed Still Lifes of Oriental Antiques
item.description: Max Schödl (1834–1921) was an Austrian artist celebrated for his meticulous still life paintings, particularly those featuring opulent Orientalist objects. Active during the late 19th and early 20th c...

item.title: Oxfordshire teenager dies from meningitis as two other pupils receive treatment
item.description: The family of Lewis Waters, a teenage sixth-form student at The Henley College in Oxfordshire, have expressed their profound grief following his death from meningitis. Lewis, described by his relative...

item.title: Diageo strengthens support for African creative industry through AMVCA sponsorship
item.description: Diageo don show strong commitment to Africa creative sector by sponsoring the Africa Magic Viewers’ Choice Awards (AMVCA) wey take place recently. The company play big role for the event, supporting e...

item.title: Health Risks of Cannabis Use in Adults Over 65 According to Stanford Experts
item.description: Cannabis use among adults over 65 is increasing in the United States, yet experts from Stanford Medicine caution that today's marijuana is significantly more potent than past decades and can pose seri...

item.title: US Considers Legal Action Against Former Cuban President Raul Castro Over 1996 Plane Shootdown
item.description: The United States government is planning to indict Raul Castro, the 94-year-old former president of Cuba and brother of Fidel Castro, according to a US Department of Justice official. The exact timing...

item.title: The Miniskirt Revolution: How 1960s Fashion Redefined Women's Style and Freedom
item.description: During the mid-to-late 1960s, the miniskirt emerged as a defining fashion trend that symbolized youth, rebellion, and female empowerment. Popularized by designers like Mary Quant in London, this darin...

item.title: Karachi AIG reveals foreign links and Lahore connections in Anmol Pinky drug network probe
item.description: Karachi Additional Inspector General Azad Khan has shared important updates about the investigation into Anmol alias Pinky, who was arrested this week from her apartment in Karachi. Police and intelli...

item.title: Ben Murray-Bruce Reflects on the Collapse of His Concert Business After 1983 Coup
item.description: Ben Murray-Bruce, founder of Silverbird Group and former senator, recounted how the 1983 military coup in Nigeria, which removed President Shehu Shagari, devastated his concert promotion business. Lau...

item.title: US Undersecretary Helberg to visit PH to strengthen tech cooperation and AI projects
item.description: Jacob Helberg, the US Undersecretary of State for Economic Affairs, will visit the Philippines from May 17 to 21 as part of efforts to strengthen technology and economic cooperation under the Pax Sili...

item.title: PTA Teachers In Federal Unity Colleges Begin Nationwide Withdrawal Of Services Over Delayed Regularisation And Poor Pay
item.description: Na so e be say thousands of PTA part-time teachers wey dey work for Federal Unity Colleges all over Nigeria don start to withdraw their services from normal teaching activities. Dem dey protest di fed...

item.title: India and UAE Formalise Strategic Defence and Energy Cooperation
item.description: During Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s recent visit to the United Arab Emirates (UAE), India and the UAE finalised a framework for a strategic defence partnership. The agreement covers multiple areas, ...

item.title: A Look at Gloria Swanson's Iconic Career Through Vintage Postcards
item.description: Gloria Swanson was more than a Hollywood star; she was a model for modern celebrity, known for her dramatic reinvention from the silent film era of the 1920s to the sound era of the 1930s. In the 1920...

item.title: American influencer faces assault charge following London Tube incident
item.description: An American social media influencer has been formally charged with assault following an incident at a London Tube station. Details regarding the exact nature of the altercation have not been fully dis...

item.title: Jury Concludes Trial Between Altman and Musk Over AI Industry Dispute
item.description: The highly anticipated trial between Sam Altman and Elon Musk, key figures in the artificial intelligence industry, reached its final stages this week. Although full details of the proceedings remain ...

item.title: MP Osoro Faces Backlash at Onyonka's Mother's Funeral Over Ruto's Contribution
item.description: On Friday, 15 May 2026, South Mugirango MP Sylvanus Osoro faced boos and heckling while delivering greetings and contributions from President William Ruto and CS Aden Duale at the funeral of Richard O...

item.title: Low Data Usage of 1.6GB Monthly Hinders Tanzania Digital Vision 2050 Goals
item.description: According to the Tanzania Communications Regulatory Authority (TCRA), internet users in the country consumed an average of only 1.6GB of data per month as of December 2025. This equals about 55MB dail...

item.title: Cross River State IRS Seals Federal Institutions Over Outstanding Tax Liabilities
item.description: The Cross River State Internal Revenue Service (CRIRS) recently carried out enforcement action by sealing the Cross River Basin Development Authority and the Federal Neuropsychiatric Hospital in Calab...
